title: Async & Queued Operations
description: Developer dispatches agent prompts to Laravel queues for background processing, with broadcast notifications on completion.

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Dispatches agent prompts to queues, registers then/catch callbacks, configures queue/connection

actors.name: Laravel Queue Worker
actors.description: Picks up and executes InvokeAgent or BroadcastAgent jobs in background

actors.name: Agent (Contract)
actors.description: Underlying AI agent invoked via prompt() or stream() inside the job

actors.name: Broadcast System
actors.description: Receives StreamEvent via broadcastNow() during BroadcastAgent execution

actors.name: Test Environment
actors.description: Uses FakePendingDispatch to prevent real queue dispatch during testing

use_cases.name: Dispatch Agent Prompt to Queue
use_cases.description: Developer wraps agent+prompt in QueuedAgentPrompt DTO, dispatches InvokeAgent job via QueuedAgentResponse(PendingDispatch)
use_cases.entry_point: QueuedAgentResponse wraps PendingDispatch; developer calls ->onQueue(), ->onConnection(), ->delay() etc.

use_cases.name: Process Agent Invocation in Background
use_cases.description: Queue worker executes InvokeAgent::handle() which calls agent->prompt(prompt, attachments, provider, model) then fires thenCallbacks

use_cases.name: Stream Agent Response with Broadcast
use_cases.description: Queue worker executes BroadcastAgent::handle() which streams agent response, broadcasting each StreamEvent to configured channels, then fires callbacks

use_cases.name: Register Success Callback
use_cases.description: Developer calls ->then(Closure) on QueuedAgentResponse; callback is serialized (SerializableClosure) and stored on the job, fired after successful agent invocation
use_cases.call_chain: QueuedAgentResponse::then(cb) → PendingDispatch::getJob()->then(cb) → InvokesQueuedResponseCallbacks stores SerializableClosure in thenCallbacks[]

use_cases.name: Register Failure Callback
use_cases.description: Developer calls ->catch(Closure) on QueuedAgentResponse; callback stored on job, fired from InvokesQueuedResponseCallbacks::failed(Throwable) when job fails
use_cases.call_chain: QueuedAgentResponse::catch(cb) → job->catch(cb) → catchCallbacks[] → fired by failed(Throwable)

use_cases.name: Configure Queue Dispatch Options
use_cases.description: Developer fluently configures dispatch: onQueue(), onConnection(), delay(), afterCommit(), chain(), afterResponse() — all proxied through QueuedAgentResponse.__call() to PendingDispatch

use_cases.name: Fake Queue Dispatch for Testing
use_cases.description: FakePendingDispatch replaces real PendingDispatch; all queue configuration methods are no-ops, __destruct does nothing (prevents real dispatch). HasQueuedResponseCallbacks skips callback registration when FakePendingDispatch detected.
use_cases.call_chain: QueuedAgentResponse(FakePendingDispatch) → then()/catch() check instanceof FakePendingDispatch → skip → no real dispatch

key_relationships: InvokeAgent implements ShouldQueue, uses InvokesQueuedResponseCallbacks trait
BroadcastAgent implements ShouldQueue, uses InvokesQueuedResponseCallbacks trait
FakePendingDispatch extends PendingDispatch (inheritance, overrides all methods as no-ops)
HasQueuedResponseCallbacks detects FakePendingDispatch to skip callback wiring in tests
InvokesQueuedResponseCallbacks stores callbacks as SerializableClosure for queue serialization
QueuedAgentPrompt is a DTO (data carrier, no behavior) for agent+prompt+attachments+provider+model
important_notes: QueuedAgentPrompt is a plain DTO — it does NOT dispatch itself; dispatching is done externally
QueuedAgentResponse.__call() proxies ALL unknown methods to PendingDispatch for full fluent API support
BroadcastAgent uses agent->stream() not agent->prompt(); each StreamEvent is broadcast in real-time during queue processing
Callbacks are serialized with SerializableClosure (laravel/serializable-closure) to survive queue serialization
FakePendingDispatch::__destruct is a no-op, preventing Laravel's auto-dispatch on destruction
Both jobs expose displayName() returning agent class name for queue monitoring tools
